description: Very long detailed clinical narrative in request

input.message: Patient is a 67-year-old male with history of COPD, atrial fibrillation, and type 2 diabetes who presents today with worsening dyspnea over the past 3 days. He reports increased sputum production, yellowish color, and mild fever of 100.2F measured at home. He has been compliant with his tiotropium inhaler and apixaban. His metformin was recently increased. He denies chest pain, hemoptysis, or lower extremity edema. On exam, he is in mild respiratory distress, oxygen saturation 91% on room air, with diffuse wheezing bilaterally and prolonged expiratory phase. Based on this presentation, what diagnoses should I consider and what workup would you recommend?
